general.title: General System Overview
general.content:
The system is designed to manage planning and tracking of tasks within an autonomous team led by a supervisor (referred to as "master"). It enables the creation of technological cards for each task or order, distributes workload among team members, considers their qualifications and current load, and controls task completion. This approach helps to optimize workflows and reduce overloads.

The master creates a technological card (tech card), which describes the final product, the sequence of operations, and the required materials. At this stage, no specific executors are assigned — the card serves as a structured plan for the upcoming work.

Once the plan is ready, the system automatically assigns tasks to executors (referred to as "units") based on their availability and qualification level. Less experienced units are given additional time based on configured coefficients, while already busy units are spared from receiving new tasks. This helps meet deadlines without overloading the team. The master may also manually adjust the distribution if needed.

After the plan is confirmed, each task appears in the executor’s personal schedule. The task includes clear instructions on what materials to use, what to do, and the expected result, along with a calculated completion time.

When a task is completed, the executor clicks the "Done" button. If a controller is assigned in the team, the task goes to them for review. The controller either confirms the result or marks it as defective. If no controller is present, the executor marks the task as either completed or defective.

Each tech card goes through several states:

  • DRAFT — a draft plan is being formed;
  • PREPARED — ready for task assignment;
  • PLANED — tasks have been assigned to executors;
  • PERFORMED — tasks are completed and waiting for review;
  • READY — all work is done and the product is completed;
  • DEFECTED — a defect was found and correction tasks are needed;
  • CLOSED — the card is closed;
  • CANCELED — the card is canceled.

Thus, the master always has a full view of the project status, can manage task flow, adjust plans, and quickly respond to issues.
